spec.description: MongoDB Dashboard using Mongodb Exporter by percona for MongoDB deployed in Kubernetes (inspired by Opstree dashboard)

spec.title: Mongodb Uptime
spec.description: Mongodb Service Uptime in hours

spec.title: Current Connections
spec.description: The number of incoming connections from clients to the database server. This number includes the current shell session. Consider the value of connections.available to add more context to this datum.

spec.title: Available Connections
spec.description: The number of unused incoming connections available. Consider this value in combination with the value of connections.current to understand the connection load on the database, and the UNIX ulimit Settings document for more information about system thresholds on available connections.

spec.title: Total Created Connections
spec.description: Count of all incoming connections created to the server. This number includes connections that have since closed.

spec.title: Active Connections
spec.description: The number of active client connections to the server. Active client connections refers to client connections that currently have operations in progress.

spec.title: Received Insert Operations
spec.description: The total number of insert operations received since the mongod instance last started.

spec.title: Received Queries
spec.description: The total number of queries received since the mongod instance last started.

spec.title: Received Update Operations
spec.description: The total number of update operations received since the mongod instance last started.

spec.title: Received Delete Operations
spec.description: The total number of delete operations since the mongod instance last started.

spec.title: Received Command Operations
spec.description:
The total number of commands issued to the database since the mongod instance last started.

opcounters.command counts all commands except the write commands: insert, update, and delete.

spec.title: Received getMore Operations
spec.description: The total number of getMore operations since the mongod instance last started. This counter can be high even if the query count is low. Secondary nodes send getMore operations as part of the replication process.

spec.title: Total Collections/DB
spec.description: Number of collections in the database.

spec.title: Total Indexs/DB
spec.description: Total number of indexes across all collections in the database.

spec.title: Total Views/DB
spec.description: Number of views in the database.

spec.title: Allocated Storage to Collections in DB
spec.description: Sum of the space allocated to all collections in the database for document storage, including free space.

spec.title: Allocated Storage to Indexes in DB
spec.description: Sum of the space allocated to all indexes in the database, including free index space.

spec.title: OPLog Size
spec.description: The oplog (operations log) is a special capped collection that keeps a rolling record of all operations that modify the data stored in your databases.

spec.title: ReplSet Last Election
spec.description: This shows the time since the last election.

spec.title: Max Member Ping Time - $instance
spec.description: This metric can show a correlation with the replication lag value.

spec.title: Replication Lag
spec.description: MongoDB replication lag occurs when the secondary node cannot replicate data fast enough to keep up with the rate that data is being written to the primary node. It could be caused by something as simple as network latency, packet loss within your network, or a routing issue.

spec.title: Cursor Timeout
spec.description: Cursor is a pointer, and using this pointer we can access the document. cursor.timeOut property was updated incrementally, and connections that died without closing the cursor. The consequence is that it will remain open on the server and consume memory unless it is reaped by the default MongoDB setting.

spec.title: Cursor totalOpened
spec.description: Number of cursors currently opened by MongoDB for clients

spec.title: MongoDB Dashboard
